title: Fleet concurrency bug: 2 lanes share the singleton .evolve/cycle-state.json -> phase-gate confusion stalls cycles before audit + loses work
description:
LIVE FINDING (2026-07-03, 2-lane `evolve fleet --count 2` on opus, cycles 474+475): concurrency WORKED (2 child cycle processes ran in genuine parallel, both on claude/opus, disjoint scopes internal/lanestatus vs internal/router+policy, per-run leases+sockets isolated, ZERO opus exhaustion). BUT NEITHER CYCLE COMPLETED TO SHIP: both stalled after the verification gauntlet (scout->...->adversarial-review->flake-rerun-scan) and NEVER reached audit (no audit-usage.json / ship-usage.json in either run dir). `evolve cycle reset` then found cycle 474 stale at phase=coverage-gate. Fleet reported '2/2 cycles ok' (child exit=0) — MISLEADING: exit-0 != shipped. Cycle 474's ~15-file lanestatus build was in its worktree, never committed to the branch (branch +0 vs main), worktree cleaned => WORK LOST.

ROOT CAUSE (hypothesis, high-confidence): the ORCHESTRATOR/phase-gate reads the SINGLETON `.evolve/cycle-state.json` (storage.WriteCycleState, last-writer-wins) — under 2 simultaneous lanes in lockstep (both at build, both at coverage-gate: max contention, amplified by parallel_evaluate=enforce fanning out WITHIN each cycle = ~4x concurrency), lane B's cycle-state write clobbers lane A's current phase, so the phase-gate precondition check for the next transition (->audit) sees the wrong phase and refuses/stalls. The per-run `runs/cycle-N/run.json` MIRROR is isolated (CB.4) but the orchestrator's OWN transition logic keys off the singleton. This is the SAME singleton hazard the unified-status design flagged for the STATUS READER — it turns out the orchestrator has it too. Earlier trials (#1/#2) reached audit because their lanes ran STAGGERED (less simultaneous cycle-state contention); lockstep lanes expose it.

FIX: the orchestrator/phase-gate for a fleet cycle (EVOLVE_FLEET=1 / has a RunID) must read+write its PER-RUN cycle-state (runs/cycle-N/run.json mirror, already written) as the AUTHORITATIVE source, NOT the shared singleton; the singleton becomes a best-effort convenience for the sequential path only. Reuse the run.json mirror (storage.mirrorRunState) + per-run flock. Alternatively route cycle-state through runlease-adjacent per-run files. STRICT TDD: a red test simulating 2 concurrent RunCycle calls with interleaved cycle-state writes must show lane A reaching audit uncorrupted; -race; the mirror is the SSOT under fleet. VERIFY: re-run `evolve fleet --count 2` disjoint plan => BOTH cycles reach audit+ship, both merge (serialized via ship.lock), no lost work.

IMPACT: this is the PREREQUISITE for reliable 2-wide waves (fleet{count:2}). Until fixed, the drain runs SEQUENTIALLY (single loop ships reliably). HIGHEST priority — blocks the parallelization the operator asked for.
